name: ArrayList vs LinkedList for random access
question_text: In the standard Java Collections Framework, what is the expected time complexity of get(index) for an ArrayList and for a LinkedList respectively?
general_feedback: ArrayList is backed by an array, so indexed access is O(1). LinkedList is a doubly-linked list, so get(index) must walk from the nearest end, giving O(n).
correct_feedback: Correct — Array indexing vs linked traversal.

answers.feedback: Reversed — the array-backed list is the one with constant-time indexing.
LinkedList indexed access cannot be O(1) without random access.
ArrayList indexed access is O(1) because it uses an underlying array.
